target_state: Analyst and advisor users can perform required review actions or see product-native blockers. Each action creates workflow state, audit evidence and safe output without client leakage. Advisor approval remains distinct from compliance release.
global_rules: Operational UI must not expose process, gate, proof, contract or source-trace scaffolding as visible guidance.
Internal draft payloads, analyst notes, advisor notes, raw traces and debug metadata remain internal-only.
Advisor approval cannot release to client, export, unlock client visibility or satisfy client acceptance.
Advisor negative paths must keep release/export/client visibility blocked and must preserve the advisor rationale.
Missing evidence must create a request or blocker, not evidence sufficiency.

allowed_actions.negative: Must not approve advice, release content, export or create client visibility.
Must not be described or treated as advisor approval.
Must not become client rejection, compliance release block, export failure or client status.
Must fail closed without accepted scoped evidence.
Must not release to client, export, satisfy compliance release or client acceptance.

output: evidence placeholder/request item where records exist
allowed_actions.negative: Must not mark evidence sufficient or compliance released.
Must not mean compliance block, client rejection, export failure or released client-safe status.

option_comparison.target: Represented as review context and recommendation options. It does not execute advice.
option_comparison.implementation_rule: If persistence for a new comparison decision is not needed, the UI may show option comparison as read-only context, but it must not look like an execution command.

workflow_slices.name: Operational UI cleanup
workflow_slices.target: Affected analyst/advisor operational screens show product-native object state, rationale input, blocked controls and next actions only.
remove_from_visible_ui: badge clusters whose purpose is implementation proof
allow_as_non_visible_test_metadata: minimal non-user-facing attributes when tests need route anchoring

implementation_subtasks.title: Wire advisor rationale and approval gate
Replace proof/meta panels with operational state

acceptance_criteria: Advisor approval requires a non-empty advisor rationale.
Advisor request-evidence and return-to-analyst are service-backed or fail closed with clear product-native blocker.
Advisor approval remains separate from compliance release and client visibility.
Analyst draft actions do not expose internal fields to client surfaces.
Operational UI contains no visible process/gate/proof/meta explainer panels for the EPIC-4 surfaces.
